title: auth — declared entitlements
description: Six entitlement keys. Refresh-token rotation is hard-required always but the flag exists for framework rollback capability. Extended-TTL sessions + JWT extended-expiry are Enterprise-tier. Cross-app SSO is medium+. Impersonation is Stackra-internal only. New-device notifications are opt-in medium+.

entitlements.description: Extend Sanctum PAT absolute lifetime beyond the framework default (168 hours) up to auth.session.extended_ttl_days (default 90 days). Only Enterprise tier.

Hard-required always. Documented as an entitlement so a framework-level rollback flag exists for the (rare) case where a rotation regression forces us to disable rotation across all tenants without a code rollback.
Grant-based cross-Application SSO. Team+ tier. Requires auth.cross_app_sso.enabled=true globally.

Stackra-internal capability — authorises platform staff to impersonate a tenant User. Present on every plan tier because it's a SUPPORT capability, not a customer-facing feature. Every tenant can be impersonated by support with valid reason (subject to auth.impersonation.notify_impersonated_user=true).
Opt-in per-User security alerts on novel-device / novel-location logins. Team+ tier. Users on Free plans can still enable it via a per-User setting, but the entitlement gate blocks it by default.

Extend inter-service JWT lifetime beyond the framework default (900s) up to auth.jwt.extended_ttl_seconds (default 3600s). Enterprise-only, typically negotiated for enterprise-integration partners with slow token-refresh loops.
